data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $150M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector comparables in Climate & Energy, supplemented by stage-progression methodology. The comparable universe includes five private peers—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—alongside one public comp (LNZA), reflecting the nascent public market presence in this subsector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $150M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercialization progress within the peer cohort.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $242M to $408M, with a median of $320M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the substantial variance in peer valuations; peers above $300M typically demonstrate advanced technical validation or clear commercialization pathways. Key uncertainty centers on Antora's specific stage within Series B and the comparability of its technology maturity relative to higher-valued peers.
